dsl_rule.translation_notes: Entry condition 1: price tests or penetrates prior day's low. Entry condition 2: ensures this happens before testing prior day's high. Exit uses a reversal condition (price crosses above prior day's high) as author exit is silent. Hold period set to extremely short-term (5 days max). The 'buying day' qualifier is interpreted as a regime filter applied at backtest time rather than a computable condition.

backtest_rule.reason: Entry condition 1: price tests or penetrates prior day's low. Entry condition 2: ensures this happens before testing prior day's high. Exit uses a reversal condition (price crosses above prior day's high) as author exit is silent. Hold period set to extremely short-term (5 days max). The 'buying day' qualifier is interpreted as a regime filter applied at backtest time rather than a computable condition.

source_rule_text: On a buying day, enter when prices test or penetrate the prior day's low price, but only if this occurs before a test of the prior day's highs.
